blurb:
Heirloom Market BBQ is a tiny place over near Akers Mill that has developed a serious following, and once you go you'll understand why. It's basically one communal table, a counter to order from, and some of the most interesting BBQ in the Atlanta area. The twist is a Korean influence running through the menu that makes it different from every other BBQ spot around here. Good for lunch with a coworker or a casual solo stop.

The BBQ is the star, and the Korean-accented flavors set it apart from a standard meat-and-three. Expect a line during peak lunch hours because the place is small and word has gotten out.

They're only open Tuesday through Saturday, 11am to 8pm, and they're closed Sunday and Monday. Plan ahead or you'll make a wasted trip.
address: 2243 Akers Mill Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Sunday: Closed

blurb:
Canton Cooks on Roswell Road is not your standard Chinese takeout place. The menu goes well beyond the usual, with things like hot pot, BBQ duck, and lobster in black bean sauce. It's a good call when you want something a little more interesting than lo mein and fried rice, but still want a casual, no-fuss dinner.

The lobster in black bean sauce is worth trying if you want to go a little beyond the basics. It's the kind of dish that makes you realize this place is playing a different game than most Chinese spots in the area. Good for a weeknight dinner when you want to mix things up.

They're open late -- until 10pm every night -- which is convenient if dinner runs late.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Los Bravos is the kind of neighborhood Mexican place you end up at on a Tuesday night when everyone's hungry and nobody wants to think too hard. The food from the kitchen is consistently good and the prices are fair. Works well for a family dinner or a quick lunch.

The food is the reason people keep coming back, and with over 800 reviews at 4.2 stars, they're clearly doing something right. Just go in knowing that the margaritas are pricier than you might expect compared to other spots nearby. Stick to the food and you'll leave happy.
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
SATE is one of those places that feels like a genuine find. It's on Roswell Road and the owner is the kind of person who actually talks to you about the food and what makes it different. Reviewers say he explained the whole pho-making process, and that it shows in the bowl -- this is one of the few spots around that makes pho the traditional way.

The pho is the thing to get here, especially if you have never tried it before. The broth is rich and clearly made with care. Prices are very reasonable, which makes it an easy weeknight option or a quick lunch when you want something warm and filling.

They're open every day 10am to 9pm, which is more convenient than most spots like this.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is a genuine find if you haven't been in yet. It's a Brazilian açaí shop where you build your own bowl and pile on as many toppings as you want. Great for an afternoon treat with the kids or after a workout when you want something that feels indulgent but isn't terrible for you.

The self-serve açaí bowls are the main draw, but they also serve crepes and coxinha, which is a classic Brazilian fried chicken snack. The staff is friendly and they actually let customers try new recipes they're working on, which tells you a lot about the place.

They're closed Tuesdays, and they're open until 10pm most nights which is handy.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Royal Spice on Hammond Drive is my go-to when I want Indian food and want to actually enjoy the experience, not just the food. The staff is genuinely helpful and happy to walk you through the menu, which matters a lot if you are still getting familiar with Indian cuisine. It's a good spot for a relaxed dinner out, either with a date or a small group.

The entrees and appetizers both get high marks from regulars, and the servers can answer questions about what dishes are spicier or milder if that matters to your group. The atmosphere is quiet and comfortable -- not rushed, not loud.

Just be aware they close in the afternoon between lunch and dinner service, reopening around 4:30pm most nights.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you live near Roswell Road and need a reliable pizza night, JJ's is the answer. It's a no-frills spot in a strip mall, but the pizza is the real deal. Good for a casual family dinner or when you just want something delivered without any drama.

The stuffed pizza is what people are raving about lately. It's enormous and honestly one slice will fill you up. The regular pies are solid too, and the price is right for the portion size you get.

Just note they're closed on Tuesdays, so don't count on them for a Monday-night leftover plan.
address: 2211 Roswell Rd suite 116, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Ray's on the River is the place you take someone when you want to actually impress them. It sits right on the Chattahoochee off Powers Ferry, and the views from the dining room make it feel like a real occasion. This is date night, anniversary dinner, or that out-of-town guest you want to show a good time.

The kitchen focuses on Southern seafood, and with over 11,000 reviews averaging 4.6 stars, people are not showing up here by accident. It's on the pricier side, so go in with that expectation, but most people feel it's worth it for a special night out.

Sunday hours are split, so check before you go. They close between 4pm and 5pm on Sundays before reopening for dinner.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
If you need a solid breakfast or brunch spot that won't let you down, J. Christopher's in Sandy Springs is the answer. It's the kind of place where you can bring the whole family on a Saturday morning and everyone leaves happy. Open every day but only until 2pm, so it's strictly a daytime thing.

They do the classics well -- omelets, burgers, and seasonal specials that rotate in and keep things interesting. Nothing fancy, just good food done right. The kind of meal that actually holds you over until dinner.

Just keep in mind they close at 2pm every day, so don't show up expecting a late lunch.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Sunnyside Pizzeria is the kind of neighborhood pizza place you wish every neighborhood had. It's in a shopping center off Sandy Springs Circle, but people drive from all over Atlanta for it. Good for a casual weeknight dinner, a Friday night with the family, or really any time you just want great pizza without a lot of fuss.

The Hot Honey Charred pizza is what people keep coming back for, and the Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings are worth ordering as a starter. Reviews rave about dipping the crust in the leftover wing sauce, which tells you everything you need to know. The Caesar salad is also a solid call if you want something simple and fresh alongside your pizza.

They're closed Mondays, so plan accordingly.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
